_note: Past reports, scored against the actual print. expected = pre-report trade average; actual = USDA print. Leave 'surprise' blank to let the build script score it (within 2% = in line, below estimate = bullish, above = bearish), or set it explicitly. Add a new block after each release.

history.reaction: The report's one real surprise: new-crop wheat carryout cut to 744M — 21M below the trade and 18M under May — on a smaller Hard Red Winter crop (all-wheat production trimmed to 1,543M, yield 47.0). Bullish wheat; farm price cut $0.50 to $6.00.
Old-crop carryout landed right on the trade guess — a non-event for corn.
New-crop carryout nudged up fractionally on old-crop demand tweaks; essentially unchanged at $4.40.
Old-crop beans unchanged from May and on top of the trade — no reaction.
New-crop beans held at 310, a hair under the trade — quietly friendly but no fireworks.
Old-crop wheat held at 935, just under the trade guess.
Smallest winter wheat crop in six decades on Southern Plains drought - July KC wheat ran limit-up and futures hit 2-yr highs.
Trade looked for a higher carryout; the tighter print pushed Nov beans to a 2-yr high near $12.
New-crop carryout landed near the trade guess - ample supply into 2027 capped the upside.
Old-crop carryout close to the trade estimate - little reaction.
Old-crop carryout in line - the week's move came from the new-crop sheets.
